tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but without evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). There is also hypocalcemia, hyperchloremia, and elevated urinary ketones. HbA1c is unexpectedly low. Findings are consistent with chronic kidney disease, possibly with acute component; further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
